title: Nominations Sent to the Senate for Various Federal Positions
summary: The White House announced nominations sent to the Senate, including Jeffrey Brodsky, of Florida, to be Governor of the United States Postal Service for a term expiring December 8, 2029; Adam Cassady, of Virginia, to be Ambassador at Large for Cyberspace and Digital Policy; Kaveh Farzad, of Maryland, to be Assistant Secretary of Energy for International Affairs; and William Gallo, of Florida, to be a position not shown in the provided excerpt.

title: Presidential Proclamation Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payments Problems
summary: This proclamation, issued by the President of the United States, asserts constitutional and statutory authority to impose a temporary surcharge on imports. It directs the application of the surcharge to address fundamental international payments problems and describes the scope and duration of the surcharge as authorized by the President under title 3 of the United States Code, sections 122 and 301, and section 604. The action formalizes the legal basis and purpose for implementing the temporary import surcharge as part of presidential powers and actions.

title: Continuation of the Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries
summary: The action extends the government's suspension of duty-free de minimis treatment for goods imported into the United States from all countries. It invokes authorities including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and the Trade Act of 1974 to maintain that shipments under the de minimis threshold remain subject to duties. The measure remains in effect for the period designated by the action and may be extended or modified by future actions.

title: Executive Action Terminating Certain Tariff Actions
summary: The President issues a presidential action terminating certain tariff actions that were previously in effect. Citing authority under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), the National Emergencies Act (NEA), and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974 (19 U.S.C. 2483), the action directs relevant federal agencies to implement the termination of the specified tariff measures and to take any necessary steps to reflect the change in policy. The action is published on the White House website under presidential actions in February 2026.

title: Executive Order to Promote National Defense by Ensuring an Adequate Domestic Supply of Elemental Phosphorus and Glyphosate-Based Herbicides
summary: This executive order authorizes the use of authorities under the Defense Production Act of 1950 and 3 U.S.C. 301 to help ensure an adequate domestic supply of elemental phosphorus and glyphosate-based herbicides. It states the policy aims to strengthen national defense by securing reliable access to critical agricultural inputs and stabilizing related supply chains. The order directs federal agencies to conduct a comprehensive assessment of current domestic production, imports, and supply chain vulnerabilities for these materials; identify critical suppliers and potential bottlenecks; and develop measures to address gaps, including planning for expansion of production capacity and distribution. It establishes interagency coordination and reporting requirements to monitor progress and implement prioritized production and allocation as needed to prevent shortages in defense and agricultural sectors.

title: Proclamation Honoring George Washington's Birthday and the 250th Anniversary of American Independence
summary: A presidential proclamation designates George Washington's Birthday as a national observance and acknowledges Washington's leadership in the American Revolution and his role in the founding of the United States. It notes that 2026 marks the 250th anniversary of American independence and places the observance within the broader commemorative context, describing the historical significance of Washington and the nation's intent to honor his contributions to liberty and the nation's founding.

title: Modification of Tariff Duties in Response to Threats from the Government of the Russian Federation
summary: This presidential action uses authorities including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974 to modify the duties (tariffs) applied to goods associated with the Government of the Russian Federation. The changes are designed to address threats to the United States arising from actions by the Russian government. The action specifies the legal basis, scope, and implementation steps for carrying out the modifications, and directs applicable agencies to implement and enforce the changes according to established procedures and timelines.
